description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and weathered surface, featuring a mix of earthy brown, reddish, and tan hues. The rock has a rough, pitted texture with several crevices and natural indentations, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for a long time. Its form is asymmetrical, with one side appearing more angular and the other more rounded, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. The coloration is mottled, with darker patches and lighter streaks, possibly from mineral deposits or weathering. The surface is matte and non-reflective, with a tactile hardness typical of stone. This rock would blend seamlessly into urban parks, landscaped areas, or as a natural barrier along sidewalks or trails.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped brown rock with a rough, pitted surface and mottled earthy coloration. Its natural, weathered appearance makes it suitable for urban landscaping or as a barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock, with visible lighter streaks and a prominent surface indentation.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's angular side and deeper crevices, with more pronounced dark brown and reddish tones.
description_view_2: The rock appears more rounded and compact from this side, with a smoother surface and fewer visible indentations.
description_view_3: This view reveals a mix of both angular and rounded features, with mottled coloration and a slightly sloped top surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
